title: Cota × ochroleuca
intro: Cota × ochroleuca, also known as Mountain Everlasting, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Cota × ochroleuca include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Everlasting include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cota × ochroleuca is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Cota, which is a group of flowering plants in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. The '×' symbol indicates that it is a cross between two different species. Specifically, Cota × ochroleuca is a hybrid between Cota tinctoria and Cota spectabilis. This classification is based on its genetic makeup and morphological characteristics. It is commonly known as the 'yellow dandelion' due to its yellow flower color.
uses: Cota × ochroleuca is not widely documented for its medicinal uses. However, it is a hybrid of two wildflowers that can be found in different parts of the world. Some studies suggest that certain plants in the same family as Cota × ochroleuca, such as the daisy family (Asteraceae), have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
